title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
claim: The active parent topology remains in conflict with the authoritative phase tree.
recommendation: Regenerate the parent phase map from the authoritative phase tree.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Phase 000 requires unavailable predecessor and rename-map evidence
claim: Phase 000 cannot satisfy its own blocking checklist because it is the first phase, while the checklist requires landed predecessors and a rename-map hash owned by a later phase.
counterevidence: The checklist may be a reusable SOL template, but it calls itself the blocking phase-000 verifier contract and has not marked the prerequisites as conditional or not applicable.
alternativeExplanation: The program may intend the map hash to be absent at bootstrap and added later. The present wording still makes it a pre-commit obligation for phase 000.
recommendation: Make CHK-007 explicitly assert no predecessor for phase 000, remove or mark the rename-map hash as not applicable there, and require that hash only from the phase that freezes the map onward.
scopeProof: The contradiction is within phase-000 planning and verification documents; no target artifact was changed.

focus: Traceability: phase-000 bootstrap prerequisites and evidence ownership

results.evidence: F002: phase-000 scope and checklist require evidence that phase ordering makes unavailable.
CHK-007 and CHK-008 conflict with phase-000 adjacency and scope.
